author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
Hey 

I'm looking at https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/gloas/partial-columns/p2p-interface.md

Can someone please explain why we've added the slot to the GroupId in Gloas  (we only had the block root as the GroupId in Fulu ) ?

author: danielknopik
content: if you receive a partial message for an unknown block root, you can use the slot to determine whether you should start looking for that block and queue that message for reprocessing. E.g., if some node is lagging pretty hard and gossips you a message for an old slot, you can discard that message instead of starting block lookup.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: Hmm, the one problem we have now is that we no longer have the signed block header in partial columns.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: So, nodes can send us whatever block root/slot they want to us as part of the Group Id and make us spend CPU on KZG verification.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: Oh nvm, we can't verify a partial cells until we have seen the beacon block for that slot anyways. And if we already have the beacon block, we're good.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
<@106441459183423488> <@1000001410538151996> I don't think we will ever have to queue a partial column message for Gloas.

1) A node should only ever get cells from another node AFTER it has published it's parts Metadata
2) A node can only publish it's parts Metadata after it has looked up it's own EL for which it needs the KZG commitments from the block.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: As the block is the only source of KZG commitments in Gloas.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: So we should only ever get partial columns from other peers after we have the block.

author: danielknopik
content: 1) is incorrect. A node can always choose to eager push

author: danielknopik
content: 2) is also not correct. For example, Lighthouse propagates any columns it receives eagerly before getBlobs returns
